TextENGNew: The days stretch on—painful, agonizing, and so interminably long they make me dizzy—and they show no sign of ending.
changeReason: New translation captures the JPN nuances of dizziness (気が遠くなりそう) and painful/agonizing intensity absent from the original's simpler 'harsh and brutal.'

TextENGNew: The situation between my aunt and Satoko remains unchanged.
Every night, my aunt finds some trivial thing to scream at Satoko about.
changeReason: JPN specifies aunt finds trivial things (些細な何か) to yell about; original says 'for no reason,' which is a different meaning.

TextENGNew: Satoko, for her part, can't help but talk back, even though it always ends in tears.
And so my aunt's bellowing never stops.
And Satoko's crying never stops.
...When Satoko spots me, she clings to me.
She hides behind my back, trying to weather the storm of my aunt's shouting.
She turns to me—the only person she trusts—for refuge.
changeReason: New translation adds the critical JPN detail that the narrator is 'the only person she trusts' (唯一心を許す), which the original omits entirely.

TextENGNew: ...Satoko opens her heart to no one but me.
...And if I don't protect her, that would be too unbearably sad.
changeReason: JPN says 'that would be too unbearably sad' (あまりにあまりに悲しいこと); original says 'she'll be devastated' shifting the sadness from a general condition to Satoko specifically being devastated.

TextENGNew: The love and security our parents never gave Satoko—I, her only living family, have to provide.
changeReason: New translation specifies 'love and security' and identifies the narrator as 'her only living family' (唯一の肉親), closer to the JPN; original vaguely says 'that security.'

TextENGNew: Every single day.
Every time Satoko cries, every single day.
...Yesterday, when Satoko clung to me in tears.
...I was stroking her head, feeling sorry for her, when... at the same time, another emotion surged through me.
changeReason: JPN describes stroking her head (頭を撫でていて) while simultaneously feeling another emotion; original simplifies to 'patted her head' and loses the simultaneity.

TextENGNew: I wanted to grab that head, wrench her off of me, and slam her against the wall. An unbelievably monstrous impulse.
changeReason: New translation adds 'wrench her off of me' (引き剥がして) from JPN, an action the original omits. Also 'monstrous impulse' vs 'devilish and awful emotion' differs in noun.

TextENGNew: No, no, no...@k I must not think such things...!!
No—it's not that I mustn't think it. It's that I should never have become aware of it...!
changeReason: JPN distinguishes between 'mustn't think it' vs 'should never have become aware of it' (気付いちゃいけなかった); original's 'can't acknowledge' is different from 'becoming aware.'

TextENGNew: From that day onward... there was another me inside.
changeReason: JPN says 'another me' (もうひとりの僕); original says 'someone else,' which is a different entity concept.

TextENGNew: He claws at me from within my chest, trying to break through and take my place.
Whenever Satoko begs me for something through her tears,@k he forces terrible, hateful words up my throat—words that would wound Satoko beyond repair—desperate to make me spit them out.
Leave me alone.
Stop clinging to me.
You're too noisy—go away...!!
changeReason: New translation includes 'You're too noisy' (うるさいから) from JPN, which the original omits entirely.

TextENGNew: No, no, no—I can't, I can't, I can't!!!
To keep him from taking over my body, I clenched my gut... and forced him out.
...I managed to drive him out.
changeReason: New translation specifies 'drive him out' matching JPN 追い出す; original's vague 'managed to do that' loses the specific action.

TextENGNew: But driving him out was not the end of it.
changeReason: New translation explicitly states 'driving him out was not the end' matching JPN; original's 'But that wasn't it' is vague and could mean different things.

TextENGNew: ...That other me—unbelievably—has been following me ceaselessly, trying to slip back inside.
changeReason: New translation captures JPN 何ということか (unbelievably) and 常に (ceaselessly) plus identifies 'that other me' vs original's vague 'that person.'

TextENGNew: ...But I will not let something this terrifying back into my heart.
He knows, too, that as long as I keep my heart steeled like this, there is no gap for him to exploit.
......So instead of forcing his way in... he follows right behind me, always at my back, patiently, endlessly waiting for me to let my guard down...
When I walk... he follows, pattering softly behind me.
changeReason: New translation includes the JPN onomatopoeia ぺたぺた as 'pattering softly,' conveying the specific eerie sound of footsteps that the original omits.

TextENGNew: Trailing right at my back.
changeReason: JPN says 'trailing right at my back' (僕の背中を付いて来る); original says 'breathing down my neck,' which is a different idiom with different connotation.

TextENGNew: He follows everywhere, and when I lie down, he looms over me from beside my pillow.
changeReason: New translation specifies 'from beside my pillow' (枕元から) matching JPN; original's 'stares down at me' omits this specific, creepy detail.

TextENGNew: ...Waiting for the chance to slip back into my heart and hurt Satoko...
...It's gotten suddenly hotter lately.
changeReason: JPN says it got 'suddenly hotter' (急に暑くなりだした); original says 'It's been so hot' losing the sudden onset.

TextENGNew: Maybe the heat is wearing down my body and mind, and that's why these thoughts keep creeping in...
I am Satoko's only family.
...That means I have to shield her from that aunt for as long as I live...
That is my lifelong duty...
changeReason: JPN says 'lifelong duty' (一生の義務); original says 'eternal duty' which implies something different (永遠 vs 一生).

TextENGNew: Lifelong?
changeReason: JPN echoes '一生？' (lifelong?); original expands to 'the rest of my life?' adding words not present.

TextENGNew: For the rest of my life? Forever?
changeReason: JPN has two questions '生涯、永遠？' (for the rest of my life? forever?); original collapses both into just 'Forever?'

TextENGNew: Do I have to go on like this... forever...?
If only Satoko would just...
But I'm her only family...
But this is just too much...
changeReason: JPN says 'this is just too much' (いくらなんでも); original's bare 'But...' loses the specific meaning of excess/impossibility.

TextENGNew: It's because of that aunt.
changeReason: JPN says 'that aunt' (叔母が) with demonstrative distance; original says 'my aunt' which is neutral. New translation's 'that aunt' captures the distancing tone.

TextENGNew: But if I'm being honest, the villagers are just as much to blame...!
changeReason: New translation captures the JPN exclamatory accusation and the nuance of sharing blame (それを言ったら...だって); original's calm 'But it's because of the villagers too' lacks the emotional force and the conditional logic.

TextENGNew: Watanagashi is almost here.
Some irresponsible villagers are saying the curse is sure to strike again this year.
And if it does, they say it will fall upon me and Satoko.
changeReason: JPN has a conditional structure 'if the curse strikes, they say it will fall upon me and Satoko' (祟りがあるなら); original states it more directly as certainty.

TextENGNew: ...Maybe it's just my imagination, but lately I can feel the stares of the villagers who "expect" it to happen—and those stares cut especially deep...
I have this feeling I'm going to be killed on the day of Watanagashi.
changeReason: JPN says 'baseless, formless paranoia' (根拠なき漠然とした妄想); original says 'just my imagination' which is much milder and loses the paranoia aspect.

TextENGNew: Of course, this is nothing but a baseless, formless paranoia.
Ah, pat, pat, pat, pat.
Don't come into my heart...
changeReason: JPN says 'don't come into my heart' (僕の心に); original says 'inside of me' which is vaguer than the specific heart reference.

TextENGNew: Satoko is my only sister, my only family—so stop, stop trying to whisper those terrible things into my heart...
Pat, pat, pat, pat—stay back, stay back, don't come near me...!!!
changeReason: New translation changes 'don't come this way' to 'stay back, don't come near me' — different directive actions that alter the reader's mental picture.

TextENGNew: Those footsteps drew close behind me, and a hand reached for my shoulder...!!!
"Wah!@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1045. ......Oh... it's you, Rena..."
changeReason: Original uses standard double-backtick quotation marks; new translation uses standard quotation marks. Also 'Aaaah!' vs 'Wah!' are different exclamations, and new adds 'it's you, Rena' specifying recognition.

TextENGNew: "A...... Are you okay?@k@vS20/02/VTK1_rena1002. ...Are you?"
Apparently I had been acting strangely enough for others to notice.
changeReason: New translation adds 'enough for others to notice' (人が見てもわかるくらい) from JPN; original's 'I must have been acting weird' doesn't convey that outside observation aspect.

TextENGNew: ...Rena was looking at me with a worried expression.
...Her name is Rena Ryuugu.
She just moved here recently as a transfer student.
Nobody remembers her, but apparently she lived in Hinamizawa a long time ago.
Though she herself says she barely remembers anything about it.
With Mion being the only other classmate close to my age, Rena has become a very precious friend.
changeReason: New translation identifies Rena specifically as 'a very precious friend' (とても貴重な友人) and restructures to match JPN; original's 'I'm glad she's my friend' is vaguer.

TextENGNew: She usually carries on happily alongside Mion, but now and then she shows a more thoughtful, feminine kind of consideration.
"Are you not feeling well?@k@vS20/02/VTK1_rena1004. Maybe you should rest in the nurse's office.@k@vS20/02/VTK1_rena1005....Don't you think?"
changeReason: JPN asks '具合悪いの' (are you not feeling well) and suggests resting; original offers 'Do you want me to take you' which adds the offer of accompaniment not in JPN.

TextENGNew: "Thanks.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1047. I'm fine.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1048. ...Did I really look that bad?"
"Yeah."
Her unhesitating answer told me just how obviously unwell I must have appeared.
changeReason: New translation specifies 'how obviously unwell I must have appeared' (具合を悪そうにしていた) from JPN; original's 'I wasn't myself' is vaguer.

TextENGNew: "......Is it... about Satoko-chan? Are you worried?"
"No... that's not it."
She had gone straight for the heart of it—sensing that my malaise was not physical, but emotional...
changeReason: New translation captures JPN's 核心を突いて来る (went straight for the heart of it) and the distinction that malaise was emotional not physical; original simplifies to just 'She knows.'

TextENGNew: ...This is just my intuition, but beneath Rena's gentle exterior, she may be far more perceptive than she appears...

"...Since I just transferred here, I might not be very reliable...@k@vS20/02/VTK1_rena1009. But I can listen.@k@vS20/02/VTK1_rena1010....I think even just talking about it can lighten the weight a little."
Despite having only recently transferred in, she seemed to have already picked up on the situation surrounding us Houjou siblings and the village dynamics.
changeReason: New translation adds 'us Houjou siblings' and specifies 'picked up on the situation surrounding' (敏感に感じ取っている) matching JPN's more nuanced phrasing vs original's simpler 'knows quite a bit.'

TextENGNew: But... talking about it would solve nothing.
No—worse than that...
changeReason: JPN says 'No—worse than that...' (いや、むしろ); original just says 'Actually,' losing the escalation and negative implication.

TextENGNew: If I spoke of it, my heart might acknowledge its own weakness,@k and that might give the other me the opening he needed to creep back inside...

"You were really startled just now when I called out to you, weren't you?@k@vS20/02/VTK1_rena1012. If you were lost that deeply in thought, then whatever's bothering you must be really serious...@k@vS20/02/VTK1_rena1013.Hauu..."
changeReason: New translation adds 'weren't you?' and 'If you were lost that deeply in thought' matching JPN's structure about degree of preoccupation, vs original's simpler version.

TextENGNew: "Oh, that was...... ahahaha.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1051. For some reason lately... I keep feeling like someone is following me.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1052. ......Pit, pit, pat, pat.
"...So I thought it was that...@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1054. Ahahahahaha, sorry.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1055.I don't even know what I'm saying anymore."
changeReason: JPN says 'So I thought it was that' (だからそれかと思って) referring to the footsteps; original says 'I thought that was you' referring to Rena, which is a different referent.

TextENGNew: "......Footsteps?@k@vS20/02/VTK1_rena1015. ...Following you...?"
"......I... I know those footsteps."
changeReason: JPN has a single statement 'I know those footsteps' (その足音、知ってる); original doubles the statement with 'I know those. I know those footsteps.' adding emphasis not in the original.
